cinematic.angle.mixed.variation_clause: from {n_word} different camera angles and shot types

lookups.mode_signal_template: This is not a technical reference {contrast_word} — these are {n_word} cinematic film captures of the same person in a consistent {setting_word}.

input_decl_intro_variants.is_single_ref: You are given a character reference image.
input_decl_intro_variants.mod_a_only: You are given two reference images of the same character.
input_decl_intro_variants.mod_b_only: You are given two reference images.
input_decl_intro_variants.mod_a_and_mod_b: You are given three reference images of the same character.

quality_anchor.cinematic:
Each panel matches the rendering quality of the source image.
Preserve material richness, lighting nuance, atmospheric depth, and surface detail across all panels.
quality_anchor.technical:
Each panel is rendered with consistent photorealistic, DSLR-level fidelity.
Preserve sharp surface detail, natural muted tones, and clean rendering across all panels.

environment_variants.Preserve: Keep the same atmospheric environment from the source image across all panels.
environment_variants.Neutral: Neutral background across all panels. Consistent backdrop tone.

forbidden_universal_pre: No text, no labels, no captions, no watermarks.
No face drift, no identity drift between panels.
No hairstyle drift, no outfit change between panels.
forbidden_universal_post: No simplification, no flat utilitarian rendering, no quality downgrade from the source image.
lookups.forbidden_mod_h_preserve_line: No replacement of the source environment with a neutral studio backdrop.

lines: Reference A provides body proportions, hairstyle, outfit, materials, and footwear.
Reference B provides the highest-authority face: facial identity, facial structure, and fine facial details.
If the references conflict, preserve the face from Reference B first, then body and outfit from Reference A, then materials.
Reference A provides body proportions, hairstyle, outfit, materials, and footwear.
If the references conflict, preserve body and outfit from Reference A, then Reference B details.
Reference A provides body proportions, hairstyle, outfit, materials, and footwear.
If the references conflict, preserve body and outfit from Reference A, then Reference B {descriptor} details.
Reference A provides body proportions, hairstyle, outfit, materials, and footwear.
Reference B provides the highest-authority face: facial identity, facial structure, and fine facial details.
If the references conflict, preserve the face from Reference B first, then body and outfit from Reference A, then Reference C details.
